rationale: The US-Iran conflict continues with another day of exchanges and an oil tanker struck — this is a continuation of an already-known situation, but the sustained Hormuz closure and tanker strikes keep indirect pressure on Polish energy prices at a meaningful level; no qualitative escalation beyond what was already tracked.

top_events.title: US-Iran: second consecutive day of strikes, tanker hit
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes for a second day in a row; three Indian sailors were killed in a US strike on an oil tanker. This is a continuation of the known conflict, not a new qualitative escalation, but sustained tanker strikes and Hormuz disruption keep indirect upward pressure on oil/energy prices in Poland.

top_events.title: UN watchdog demands Iran disclose nuclear stockpile
top_events.summary: The IAEA formally demanded Iran provide information on its nuclear stockpile during the active US-Iran conflict. This is a continuation of the already-tracked nuclear escalation vector; no new qualitative change, but the pressure remains relevant to European security.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea to strengthen bilateral ties — a continuation of the previously tracked strategic shift. Indirect NATO/Poland relevance remains, but no new concrete development.
